rulings.Case Title: GWENDOLYN CAMPBELL VS. GORDON RUBENSTEIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Sep 5, 2019 line Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction is DENIED. Moving party failed to show that under the circumstanced of this case money damages are not enough to compensate the Plaintiff and that that the balance of the hardships is in Plaintiffs favor. Additionally, no authority is cited directly on point regarding the likelihood of prevailing on the merits re: specific performance of the residential lease requiring a removal of existing residential occupants. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: PIER 39 LIMITED PARTNERSHIP VS. ZEN LOUIE LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Sep 5, 2019 line CROSS DEFENDANT PIER 39 LIMITED PARTNERSHIP MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Non-opposition filed. Cross-Complaint is dismissed without prejudice. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: PIER 39 LIMITED PARTNERSHIP VS. ZEN LOUIE LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Sep 5, 2019 line PLAINTIFF PIER 39 LIMITED PARTNERSHIP MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT is DENIED. Plaintiff's complaint for unlawful detainer appears to contain two distinct actions (1) an unlawful detainer action against tenant in possession, including damages; and (2) a breach of contract action against the guarantor. The Complaint does not clearly allege which defendant is in possession, if any. In fact, the complaint does not allege that any of the defendants failed to comply with the portion of the Notice demanding possession (Complaint alleges that "Defendants, and each of them" were served with the Notice demanding possession) and the prayer for relief does not seek possession. It may be only inferred that Plaintiff is seeking possession from the following language: "Plaintiff is entitled to possession of the Premises." However, even this inference does not clarify which Defendant is alleged to be in possession and which is not. Plaintiff's position regarding this issue is clarified in this motion. Defendant's answer was filed prior to this clarification. The answer covers includes defenses to (1) claim for possession and (2) claim for money damages against the guarantor. All these defenses are proper in light of the vagueness of the complaint. Motion to strike the exhibits serves no purpose and does not advance the case. The attachment of these exhibits to the answer does not grant them any significance. Whether these documents are attached to the answer will not change how these documents are viewed/ "examine[d] or consider[ed]" by the Court, i.e. having not been incorporated into the answer they provide no support to opposition to any pleading challenge and mere attachment to the answer does not make them admissible evidence for any evidentiary motion/hearing. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: PIER 39 LIMITED PARTNERSHIP VS. ZEN LOUIE LLC ET AL
